text.title: Employer Brand Content Strategy
text.summary: Content calendar structure, channel-specific voice rules, and employee testimonial intake pipeline.

rule.statement: Rotate four content types in a 4-week repeating cycle: employee spotlight, behind-the-scenes, culture moment, job opening promo. Every slot must trace back to a named EVP pillar.
rule.rationale: Mechanical calendars without novelty injection decay after 4-6 weeks of engagement. The 4-type rotation balances brand-building with hiring intent and gives the algorithm enough variety to avoid suppression.

list.item: Week 1: Employee spotlight (LinkedIn) — personal story tied to Growth or Purpose pillar.
Week 2: Behind-the-scenes (Instagram/video) — ≤90s, culture moment in action.
Week 3: Culture moment (LinkedIn long-form) — ≤300 words, specific event or milestone.
Week 4: Open role promo (LinkedIn + Indeed) — paired with "what we're building" context post.

rule.statement: Never use the same post copy across channels. LinkedIn requires professional framing; Instagram requires visual-first brevity; careers blog allows long-form authenticity.
rule.rationale: Channel voice mismatch produces flat copy that is suppressed by algorithms and dismissed by audiences. The same text on LinkedIn and TikTok will underperform on both.

rule.statement: Cap LinkedIn hashtags at 3-5 per post. Never stuff more than 5.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn best practice is 3-5 hashtags; more signals low-quality content and suppresses organic reach.

rule.statement: Every employee testimonial must have a signed consent form and image release before publication. Use DRAFT-only status with [NAME] [TITLE] tokens until consent is captured and confirmed.
rule.rationale: Publishing testimonials without consent exposes employees and the company to legal liability. Agents must never auto-publish; always require explicit human confirmation that consent is on file.

rule.statement: Require employee-supplied raw quotes before drafting testimonials. Never synthesize quotes from job-offer letters, LinkedIn profiles, or performance reviews.
rule.rationale: AI-generated testimonials that were not said by real employees are ethically indefensible and legally risky if used in candidate-facing materials.

antipattern.description: Repurposing one testimonial format across all channels without adaptation.
antipattern.reason: A 300-word LinkedIn testimonial becomes unreadable on Instagram; a 30-second video quote loses context in a blog post. Repurpose with channel-specific editing, not copy-paste.

text.reference: 4-week brand content plan with per-slot hook, body, CTA, asset brief.
Employee testimonial intake: raw quote fields, consent checklist, approval flow.
